# PCAP fixtures

Captures used to validate the decoders against real-device wire formats.

## ⚠️ Privacy: never commit unsanitized customer data

A raw capture of NetFlow/IPFIX traffic contains **real exporter and subscriber
IP addresses** — both in the IP/UDP headers and inside the flow records. This is
customer data and must **never** be committed.

Before a capture goes anywhere near git, run it through the sanitizer, which
deterministically rewrites every IPv4 address (headers **and** flow-record
fields, template-aware for v9/IPFIX) into the `10.0.0.0/8` range:

```bash
pcapsanitize --in real-capture.pcap --out fixture-sanitized.pcap
```

The sanitizer drops any v9/IPFIX data packet whose template it has not seen, so
an undecodable record can never leak a real address. It never prints original
addresses.

## What may live here

- **Synthetic** captures generated by the test suite / `benchgen` (no real data).
- **Sanitized** captures whose addresses have all been rewritten to `10.x`.

## Generating / using fixtures

```bash
# capture from a device (root), then sanitize:
sudo scripts/capture-device.sh eth0 real.pcap 60
pcapsanitize --in real.pcap --out testdata/pcap/<vendor>-sanitized.pcap

# replay a fixture into a running collector:
scripts/replay-pcap.sh testdata/pcap/<vendor>-sanitized.pcap 127.0.0.1:9995 1x netflow9
```

See `docs/field-validation.md` for the full per-vendor workflow.
